curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Calculate the area of compound 2D shapes by dissecting them into familiar shapes, finding each area accurately, and combining results using clear reasoning and correct units.

verse_ality.driving_question: How can we find the area of a complex shape by breaking it into simpler parts?

knowing_world.focus: Understand area as space inside a 2D shape and learn how to find the total area of compound figures by splitting them into simpler shapes and adding areas together.
key_concepts: Area as a measure of 2D space
Summing areas (and subtracting cut-outs when needed)

human_story.focus: Connect compound-area reasoning to real work in architecture, engineering, design, landscaping, and art—where accurate area estimates support planning, costing, and building.

expressive_self.focus: Explore playful, creative ways to decompose shapes and notice that multiple valid dissections can lead to the same total area.
activities: Find more than one way to split a compound shape into simpler parts.
Describe which shapes feel most familiar to you (rectangles, triangles, circles) and why.
Compare two different decompositions that produce the same total area and explain how you know they match.

designed_world.focus: Use an interactive tool to build compound figures from simple shapes, calculate the total area from visible dimensions, and use feedback to refine accuracy and reasoning.
design_challenges: Drag and arrange simple geometric shapes to create a compound figure.
Manually calculate the total area based on the visible dimensions.
Enter your area estimate into the answer box and click “Check Answer”.
Use feedback to identify errors and improve your method.
Explain how your dissection choice made the calculation easier or harder.

regulation_supports: Sketch the compound shape on paper and label dimensions clearly before calculating.
Trace the edges with your finger and mentally ‘walk’ the boundary to understand the structure.
Pause and re-check units and dimensions before adding areas together.

pathways.name: Final Task: Create and Verify a Compound Shape
pathways.focus: Create your own compound shape, break it into smaller shapes, calculate and record each area, then verify solutions with a peer and reflect on strategies used.
products: A compound shape created using the interactive tool (screenshot or sketch).
A breakdown into smaller shapes with labelled dimensions.
Individual area calculations for each part and a correct total area.
A peer challenge: your peer’s estimate and a comparison/verification of methods.
A short reflection on which strategies helped you stay accurate.

required: Area of rectangles and triangles (and other basic shapes used in the task)
Add and subtract to combine areas
Accurate reading of lengths and dimensions
Correct use of area units (cm², m²)

builds_toward: Area and perimeter problem solving in real contexts
Surface area of 3D shapes (breaking into faces)
More complex composite areas involving circles and sectors
